title: Migrate fetchRun consumers to getRun SDK + fix hash-defect in RunArtifactDetail
intent: Clear CI-RED-004 by migrating 3 fetchRun() consumers to getRun() from SDK, fixing a latent 3-field hash-display defect in RunArtifactDetail.vue, deleting deprecated fetchRun, and correcting the fence's fictional fetchRunV2 message.

diff_articulation.what_changed: Stores migrated from fetchRun to getRun (SDK). RunDiffViewer migrated to getRun. RunArtifactDetail.vue prop type changed to RunArtifact, hash reads fixed to use nested hashes.{gcode_sha256,toolpaths_sha256,feasibility_sha256}. Flat fields not in SDK type get (as any) casts. fetchRun deleted from legacy API. Fence message corrected from fictional fetchRunV2 to actual getRun.
diff_articulation.why_not_redundant: Backend returns hybrid shape (flat for most fields, nested for hashes). Legacy RunArtifactDetail type was partially wrong - gcode_hash/toolpaths_hash/request_hash were flat but backend returns them nested. This caused silent undefined reads in the detail view. Migration to SDK's nested RunArtifact + fixing the reads fixes the latent defect.

commands_run: npm run type-check (pre-existing errors unrelated to this change)

files.intent: Delete fetchRun, add getRun import from SDK, update downloadRun to use getRun
Import getRun from SDK, change selected type to RunArtifact, use getRun in select()

verification: Store compiles, selected uses RunArtifact type

files.intent: Import getRun from SDK, change runA/runB refs to RunArtifact, use getRun in load()
Change prop type to RunArtifact, fix hash reads to use nested hashes.{gcode_sha256,toolpaths_sha256,feasibility_sha256}, cast flat fields not in SDK type
verification: Hash fields now render correctly (they were undefined before)

files.intent: Fix fence message from fictional fetchRunV2() to actual getRun() from SDK
verification: Message now points to real function
